seoTitle: Beverley Park vs Beverly Hills — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Beverley Park and Beverly Hills. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Beverley Park vs Beverly Hills: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Beverley Park and Beverly Hills.
heading: Beverley Park vs Beverly Hills — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Beverley Park, New South Wales and Beverly Hills, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Beverley Park has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Beverly Hills (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Beverley Park or Beverly Hills safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Beverley Park has a flood risk score of 44/100, while Beverly Hills scores 48/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Beverley Park scores 26/100 for bushfire risk. Beverly Hills scores 19/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Beverley Park or Beverly Hills?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Beverley Park has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.65% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
